title: LFG — Local File Guardian
subtitle: Disk management suite with self-correcting DevDrive architecture

arc_sections.title: The Problem
arc_sections.headline: Macintosh HD at 84% — and growing
arc_sections.body: A developer machine accumulates disk pressure from multiple directions: Xcode DerivedData ballooning with each build, iOS Simulator runtimes stacking up, npm and Python caches fragmenting across dozens of projects, and sparseimage-backed volumes consuming internal SSD space that should live on external storage. No single tool addresses all of these. Result: repeated emergency cleanups, corrupt volumes, lost hours.

arc_sections.title: The Solution
arc_sections.headline: 9 specialized modules, one dispatcher
arc_sections.body: LFG routes disk management to 9 purpose-built Bash modules via a central dispatcher. WTFS maps disk topology. DTF targets 6 cache categories. BTAU manages sparseimage lifecycle. DEVDRIVE orchestrates native APFS volumes with a self-correcting reconcile loop. SSD suppresses Spotlight CPU pressure. Each module is independently invokable and observable via CCEM APM telemetry.

arc_sections.title: Traction
arc_sections.headline: DevDrive v2 live — 10 stories shipped
arc_sections.body: The DevDrive v2 native APFS architecture shipped all 10 user stories (LFG-32..LFG-41) across 5 waves. Self-correcting reconcile loop runs every 5 minutes via LaunchAgent. macFUSE 5.2 FSKit overlay proof-of-concept validated the unified /Developer namespace approach. Native Swift viewer serves the showcase at localhost:8080.

arc_sections.title: Vision
arc_sections.headline: FSKit overlay — unified /Developer namespace
arc_sections.body: Fleet formation fmt-lfg-fleet-20260419 ships showcase v2 + DevDrive remediation as a 17-story, 42-point dual-squadron build. Sparseimage migration to YJ_MORE reclaims ~27GB of internal SSD. CoreSimulator/Devices moves to DDRV901. APM gains a volume health widget. Long-term: FSKit overlay eliminates symlinks entirely with a transparent /Developer namespace.

sections.heading: DevDrive v2 — Why
sections.body: Current DevDrive uses hdiutil sparseimage wrappers. Problems: monotonic growth (never shrinks automatically), opaque allocation (host sees one big file), double-buffered cache, no drift detection hooks, band files in sparsebundles are individually corruptible. Observed pathology: disk fluctuates 50GB -> 20GB -> 30GB during dev sessions — root cause is APFS purgeable space management racing compile-time writes, compounded by sparseimage inability to release space.

sections.heading: DevDrive v2 — How
sections.body: Move to native APFS volumes in the root container via diskutil apfs addVolume -quota. Shared free-space pool. Native snapshots, CoW, TRIM. No mount/attach dance — volumes are always present. Reconcile loop reads authoritative state from diskutil. Rejected macFUSE (kext deprecated on Apple Silicon), fuse-t (NFS shim latency), ZFS (out-of-tree installation friction) in favor of kernel-native APFS.

sections.heading: DevDrive v2 — Reconcile Loop
sections.body: Observe → Classify → Act → Record. Eight drift categories map to deterministic fix actions: MISSING_LINK (recreate), STALE_TARGET (repoint), REAL_DIR_DRIFT (move to volume + link), UNMOUNTED_VOL (mount or alert), OVERSIZED (prompt or shrink), BAND_BLOAT (schedule compact for legacy), SNAPSHOT_LOCKED (thin if pressure), SWAP_PRESSURE (escalate to menubar). All actions idempotent and safe to call on every loop tick. Runs via LaunchAgent io.lfg.devdrive-reconcile.
